top.title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
top.summary: But it isn’t doing it alone; for a month starting this week, it will deploy two oblong neon submersibles as the project’s special agents, sending them nearly 6,000 meters down to hop along the seafloor. If all goes well, the test could help establish the vehicles as a tool for government agencies, scientists, and companies that hope to probe the vastly understudied deep sea and the resources it holds. Related Story These deep-sea “potatoes” could be the future of mining for renewable energy Read next Scientists and companies have long sought to probe the deep sea and bring such treasures to the surface.

Modelos.title: Musk and Altman face off in trial that will determine OpenAI's future
Modelos.summary: Musk and Altman face off in trial that will determine OpenAI’s future Musk’s shifting stance on AI dangers may complicate trial over OpenAI’s mission. OpenAI suspects that Musk is using the litigation as a delay tactic while his own AI firm, xAI— recently folded into SpaceX —races to catch up to OpenAI’s lead after the launch of ChatGPT in 2022. But the lawsuit is about much more than a couple billionaires’ big egos.

Investigación.summary: By Jessica Hamzelou archive page May 1, 2026 Stephanie Arnett/MIT Technology Review | Adobe Stock This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of around $9 billion. The foundation’s efforts were overseen by a board of 22 prominent scientists.

Mercado.title: De la IA no se salva ni Dios, así que el Vaticano ha pasado de los mandamientos a ser el primer Estado en legislarla
Mercado.summary: Y no lo hace desde la ingenuidad, sino desde una convicción teológica firme y concreta: que la dignidad humana no es negociable, ni siquiera ante un modelo de lenguaje con mil millones de parámetros. de Luis Editor Senior Linkedin twitter 3677 publicaciones de Eva R. Una institución con más de 2.000 años de antigüedad y chapada a la antigua está dando una lección de agilidad institucional a gobiernos, parlamentos y hasta a empresas tecnológicas a las que no saben por dónde les da el aire.

Mercado.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
Mercado.summary: Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review's EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought with AI at its core, not layered on after the fact. About the speaker Tarique Mustafa, Cofounder, CEO, and CTO, GC Cybersecurity Tarique Mustafa is Cofounder and CEO/CTO of two AI-powered cybersecurity companies: GCCybersecurity, Inc.

Mercado.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
Mercado.summary: The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. This conversation from MIT Technology Review's EmTech AI conference examines how AI factories unlock new levels of scale, sustainability, and governance—positioning data control as a strategic imperative for governments and enterprises. About the speakers Chris Davidson, Vice President, HPC & AI Customer Solutions, HPE Chris Davidson is Vice President of HPC & AI Customer Solutions at Hewlett Packard Enterprise.

A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
Mercado.summary: By James O'Donnell archive page May 1, 2026 Stephanie Arnett/MIT Technology Review | Adobe Stock A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners. It’s also rolling out a filter on sexual content aimed at blocking material related to gender and trans issues, which will be optional but turned on by default across all plans.
